name: Anderton, Thomas Richard
motto: He hath a lean and hungry look
description: Tom has been with us the same time that the rest have, but he's so long himself that it seems longer. In fact, one of his fellow-grafters in the signal platoon was heard to say once that Tom always reminded him of going away—"so long." Cheer up, Tom, if it wasn't for you the Jit would fail up.

name: Basch, Goldie
motto: I am never merry when I hear sweet music
description: When Goldie came to N. H. C. from the pastoral valley of Ashuelot, she was as innocent and credulous a little Freshman as you ever saw, but a year of college life made her so suspicious that she had only one remark for every occasion—"I don't believe it! I bet you're kidding me!" Fortunately, Goldie lost none of her originality in the process of sophistication. Her conversational style hasn't changed a bit—it still has the spontaneity of an O. Henry story. But unless you favor Germany, you'd better be careful how you mention the European situation, for Goldie doesn't like to discuss the war with "people that are prejudiced."

name: Batchelour, Vance Whiting
clubs: Assistant News Editor The New Hampshire (2)
description: Rass is a sly coon. He dodged the dogs for a year but finally got chased up a "Bee" tree, and has been treed ever since. Batch's chief joys in life are his horse, his fiddle, and Billie Grove's courses. He spends most of his spare time in kidding Bill into thinking that he does not get his share of the honey. If it wasn't for the letters Batch writes to us in the summer vacations, we'd think from his life here that he was an awful innocent guy.

name: Bennett, Frank Powers
motto: It's worth it
description: Yank found Carnegie too slow and so he came back to New Hampshire. Here his knowledge of "Hops" procured for him the position of marshal at the Sophomore festivities. Frank saw Vernon Castle do a new Fox Trot while in New York this summer, and he is now giving exhibitions for Bud's benefit. Bud hasn't decided to take lessons yet, but he is thinking seriously of—but what does Yank care. He says Columbia is nearer Garden City and the water isn't so rusty there.

name: Blanchard, Phyllis Mary
motto: My mind to me a kingdom is
description: Phyllis is the kind you often read about in books, but seldom meet in real life. She can discuss a profound philosophical subject in a way that shows more than ordinary insight, and with equal fervor can dramatically relate the adventures of Ignatz and Krazy. She has always been interested in nature, and last year specialized in Groves, but now has devoted all her energies to the study of psychology. We look for big things from Phyllis in this career, because personality and intellect like hers are bound to carry their possessor into the front ranks of life.

name: Bond, Charles Chester
motto: Then fill me with the old familiar juice, methinks I might recover by and by
description: One bright morning in September, there dropped into the calm and peaceful community of Durham a disciple of "God's Country." Jug immediately began to make it known that he was around. His room in "The Cockroach Institute" was made the headquarters of the uninitiated and the ignorant; and many a social gathering, not sanctioned by the Y. M. C. A., took place there. In his Sophomore year Jug tried to forget that his interest in "Bunnies" was all in vain. Now Jug has adopted the pure and simple life and the height of his ambition is to shine in the Military Department.

name: Boutwell, Leroy Arthur
description: Bout's first step to prominence was as the star rough-houser of Rand's Frat; now Bout gets his notoriety as the only guy that ever learned anything in Pat Cahill's forge shop classes. That he'll never be able to live down, though he tries to explain it by saying that he had forge shop before he came here. Well, perhaps he had; he knew an awful lot of forge shop.

name: Brown, Gladys Alice
motto: Gentle of speech, beneficent of mind
description: Gladys entered N. H. C. with the Class of 1918, but she pestered the faculty until they gave her credit for her year's work at the Boston Training School, and she is now a full-fledged Junior. Welcome, "Gladys." This is the way she divides her time: Worrying about flunking, ¼; Studying, ¼; College activities, ¼; Friendship, ¼. The first item on the schedule has never worked out properly, for no matter how hard she worries, she never manages to flunk. This is a constant source of surprise to Gladys. Her system for friendship seems to have been the most efficient of all, for hers is the sympathetic nature that makes many friends, and never loses them.

name: Burleigh, Arthur Samuel
description: For the last two years this adopted son of Charlie Wentworth has been assistant traffic manager for the Busted and Mangled Railroad at Durham Junction. Burleigh thinks he's a wireless shark, but he and Nelson have it fixed up so that one sends messages to the other who claims he's receiving from Honolulu or the Eiffel Tower or Dover or some such faraway place.

name: Burpee, Louise Elizabeth
motto: We have been friends together in sunshine and in shade
description: If you're looking for data on Manchester, ask Louise--she's absolute authority, for she keeps up to the minute by frequent trips home, and by diligent perusing of the Daily Mirror and American. For two years Louise and Henrietta have been inseparable companions, all the girls will tell you there is no more faithful friend than Louise Burpee. "Preparedness" is her motto, and she always finds time to prepare each lesson without missing any good times.

name: Carlisle, Sumner Landry
motto: Up Exeter
description: Wishing to get a little experience in social ways before attempting to dazzle the co-ed's, attended a function at Dover. The next morning one of the young ladies present was heard to ask, "Was last night the first time that Carlisle was ever out in society?" A splendid politician and promoter and originator of the famous "bag of salt" joke, ex-laundryman, etc., etc., he has sufficient experience to enable him to round out an honorable and illustrious career.

name: Caswell, Henry Benson
description: Hooky doesn't do or say much around college, for he is only happy when he is playing (?) in the band or driving "her" car. Marion is nice, we think, to trust him with one whole car. They say he can drive it one-handed, too. We don't see what a normal man with two hands wants to drive a car one-handed for, but we suppose Hooky knows what he's doing.

name: Chase, Marion Olive
motto: But there's nothing half so sweet in life As love's young dream
description: As a Freshman Chasey had no more than arrived at New Hampshire when she found herself in a whirl of social activities. At Glee Club, in dramatics and at informals and house-parties, Chasey was always there, and with the same one twice? "Hardly ever." But this all ended when Charlie carried her off in his little Ford, and made her forget the man she left behind her. When there is a discussion of love in psych. class, Chasey discourses in words of wisdom gathered from her varied experiences, but Charlie shines brightest in an analysis of jealousy. Fortunately, Chasey takes no offense at our remarks for good nature is one of her characteristics.

name: Clark, Malcolm Barrett
motto: A lion-hearted warrior
description: Fluffy tried to take the aggie course, but failed absolutely in a man's course and turned to Arts and Science. He never goes out after dark and is only seen by ferreting him out at his room. He goes to classes fifteen minutes early and is now doing advanced work in Geology under the guidance of "Batch."

name: Colby, Rachel Clarenda
motto: A good companion and as firm a friend
description: Rachel is the optimist of the Class of '17, and although practical jokes are her delight, she laughs just as heartily when the joke is on her. Underneath all her fun, however, is a determination to be square, and a consideration for others which makes her an ideal friend. That Rachel has a capacity for hard work, too, is evinced by the fact that she frequently indulges in the desire to do her housework long after the rest of the dorm is fast asleep.

name: Colomy, James Daniel
motto: My lips are virgin to a woman's kiss
description: As soon as Jim found some dry clothes after that dog party, he started in drawing up a charter for the New Hampshire College Co-ed Investigation Bureau Ltd., J. D. Colomy, general manager; A. R. Morgan, consolation prize for discards; all business to be carried on under Ma's chaperonage. Whereas, heretofore, students soliciting companionship were obliged to rely upon their own judgment or else go to Dover, now a competent, experienced, and conscientious corporation gives all desired information and advice.

name: Cooley, Leon Weston
motto: Glow, little glow-worm, glimmer, glimmer
description: Glow-worm entered college with the '16 class, but decided to drop out and wait until N. H. C. became more charitable toward "Torchies." He started in again the next year and found his chance to shine. As a musician he is quite accomplished, especially on the fiddle, his masterpiece being "Pop Goes the Weasel" and "Three Cheers for the Red, White, and Blue" for an encore. We wish Glow-worm the best of luck, for his faithfulness and cheerful nature deserve their reward.

name: Crouch, Leon Meader
description: Leon has had a hard time trying to decide which class he really likes best, but he showed his good judgment by sticking out until 1917 came along. Sweet cider, a woman, and a song now and then have been the ruin of many a man. Crouchy has cut the song and is gradually going home earlier nights; but according to Doc Whittemore, these are the first symptoms of Unity.

name: Cutts, Elmer Frank
motto: "When I beheld this I sighed, and said within myself: 'Surely mortal man is a broom-stick.'" – (Swift)
clubs: Assistant News Editor, The New Hampshire (2)
description: In the fall of 1913 "Slaps" jarred across Durham's boundary line, labeled as a man with "something to him," and speaking in terms of length, not a one has disputed it. Admired by the co-ed, boss of THE GRANITE, pride of The New Hampshire, and Secretary of Pat Cahill's Union, this 75 inches of wit, wisdom and wickedness can be recognized anywhere, for– He is a laddie, A bony, bony laddie, He's as thin as the paper on the wall. He's as light as a feather And his knees they knock together, And he is just as bright as he is tall.

name: Degnan, Henry William
motto: "Up to Dartmouth"
description: Henry is a dealer in banners and cigar safes. "Please don't put that in, fellows, I want to show it to my girl in Nashua." The only record "Hymen" ever broke is Over The Hills to Mary. Edison Henry gained national reputation as a reporter for Foster's Democrat by his thrilling words– "while climbing a sixty-foot pole." Deg's puns are quite original– with Puck, Life, and the Police Gazette.

name: Dudley, William Allen
motto: "Swans sing before they die, 'twere no bad thing Did certain persons die before they sing"
description: Take a look at this fellow and you will have to admit that he is a regular guy. His one failing is that he is an engineer. Like all true engineers, he specializes in Dover 55, and Boston 56, gets his associates to write up his reports, and then claims that he has a full schedule. He fails to say what it is full of, but from his Mexican disposition we can guess at that. His greatest accomplishment is the captaincy of the Ditch-Diggers Bowling Team. His only regret is that he rooms with a Sod Buster and an Arts and Sewing Circle man.

name: Dustin, Clifton Henry
motto: I surrender all
description: Rochester undoubtedly surrendered a genius when it turned "Cliffie" loose in this cruel world. "Cliffie" first surrendered, along with many others, to the Engineering Course, but later surrendered to the Mechanic Arts. As far as we can learn "Cliffie" has yet to surrender to a co-ed. He stands strictly apart from the fairer sex, and claims that there is a reason. Generously surrendering high military honors to others, he joined the Band of Grafters, which event marked a decided decline in the efficiency of that organization. Although he never attempts to start an argument with any of the Profs., we still believe that "Cliffie" is getting his heart's desire, "a real education."

name: Evans, George Everett
description: Georgie is our Exeter Sport, for half the time he commutes, a fourth of the time he lives in Durham, and the rest of the time even he himself does not know whether he's here or somewhere else. In the course of his college life Ev has learned how to smoke cigarettes, cut classes, wear sport clothes, and make the Glee Club.

name: Ewer, Natalie Drew
motto: The way to win a friend is to be one
description: Nat is famous for taking courses in which she is the only girl in class—Physics lab., for example. In this course she was not so successful as in physical culture, in which she won the prize which the Lieut. offered. That independence of thought is one of her strong points is shown by the brave way in which she voices her convictions in psych. 55. Loyal and energetic to the nth degree, Natalie has made firm friends throughout the whole college.

name: Fall, Beatrice Louise
motto: Or light or dark, or short or tall, She sets a springe to snare them all.
description: To judge from her many admirers, "B" must believe that "Variety is the spice of life." Although when she first came to college, she thought dancing was her forte, "Billy" soon convinced her that psychology is her real vocation, and she now regards dancing and flirting as mere pastimes. No one ever has a chance to feel left out when "B" is around, for she always has a smile and a hearty greeting for us all.

name: Fernald, Alice
motto: Her joys in life were only three—Her math, her psych, her chemistry.
description: Alice is one of Frankie's star pupils, and unlike Isabel, she couldn't think of neglecting her beloved books for a mere man. Surely a career as Dean of Women would be just the one for her.

name: Fernald, Isabel Jane
motto: Her voice was ever soft, Gentle, and low—an excellent thing in woman.
description: When Isabel took Latin I and Home Ec. 3 the same year she certainly demonstrated that the way to a man's heart is through his stomach. But we all know that Isabel's sweet and gentle nature will do as much as her excellent housekeeping toward making Pinky happy.

name: Fitch, Frank Williams
motto: 'Twas surprising one small head could carry all he knew
description: "Fish" takes the Aggie course because, he says, "The country girls appreciate a college chap." At any rate, he gets his full quota of letters and boxes of home-made fudge each week, all bearing the same postmark. That all like substances repel, Is a saying sad but true; And such it seems must be the case When woodshop doesn't suit you.

name: Frisbee, Joseph Elliot
motto: You never can tell
description: Nobody could tell why the Portsmouth mystery boy went home every Saturday night, until last summer, when the secret was revealed. He nearly flunked Math. owing to his lack of interest in "figures;" nevertheless Joe has system and we will trust to his ability to make good. Joe hadn't planned to take the engineering trip until he was elected treasurer of the Engineering Society; now he thinks he can "manage" it.

name: Gillmore, Ralph Harold
description: "Rosie" transferred to New Hampshire from Colby in order to be closer to "the ideal of his dreams," and also to take advantage of the excellent training given by our illustrious "Wood Shop Butcher." As a social chap, "Rosie" is a leader, as is substantiated by a frequent visitor who hails from the metropolis of Concord. Bits of conversation which have been overheard during these periodic trysts, lead us to believe that Harold is not as quiet as our first impression indicated. However, we heartily welcome our rosy-cheeked Concord lad, and we wish him the best of luck in this, his second choice of an Alma Mater.

name: Glidden, Chester Willis
motto: Sleep on, you fat and greasy citizen
description: This little, chubby son of the soil, has a brilliant future ahead of him. He might be called versatile, for he plays solitaire, poker, and billiards. Occasionally he sings, and oftentimes enters into a ten-round prize fight with his dearly beloved room-mate, Graham. This year he started out to find his soul-mate, and it is reported that Central Avenue furnished a hope, rather tall it is true, but you should worry, Chet, if it is correct that you are specializing in pole-beans. Congratulations, little one.

name: Graham, Roy Charles Lemach
motto: God made him, therefore let him pass for a man
clubs: Assistant Business Manager The New Hampshire (1)
description: Roy is the only member of the Junior Class with a "past;" although he proved his manhood early in his Freshman year, he has never been able to live it down. While a man of much travel and worldly experience, he came to us unstained by sordid contact with his fellow-men. Combined with this virtue, he has the business ability to manage anything in college except himself.

name: Griffin, Philip Joseph
description: After spending one year at the University of Fordham in New York City, Sloe decided to come to N. H. C. to study chemistry under the "King," and just at present is in the throes of integral calculus. Sloe has enough hours to graduate this June, but the attraction at the Dorm is so great that he cannot resist the temptation of returning for one more year; and besides he may want to drill next year. (Joke.)

name: Hardy, Verna Carola
motto: Gently to hear, kindly to judge
description: In spite of her many occupations— from book-keeping to crocheting— Verna is never in a hurry, and can always be depended upon to appreciate a joke. No one can doubt that her tastes, at least, are musical, and in Smith Hall she is famous for her vocal ability— just ask her about Les Petits Oiseaux! Unlike many of our classmates, Verna's goal has not changed— she has been true to her desire to follow in Frankie's footsteps, and to impart the principles of mathematics to the rising generations.

name: Harriman, Nathaniel Joy
motto: I care for nobody, no, not I, till somebody cares for me
description: Harrie started out to be one of the big men of the class, but he met his Waterloo in a feminine form, as so many of us have done. Now he spends most of his time week ends traveling between Exeter and Durham on the B. & M. Too bad, Doc, we are sorry for you.

name: Hatch, Frances Dorotha
motto: I am sure care's an enemy to life
description: Dorotha sharks all her courses, but we don't know how she does it, for she divides her time between Informals and the Academy dances at Exeter. Nevertheless, you'd need a stop watch to record her speed in German— she can say more words in one breath than anyone else in college. Just one request, Dorotha— before we wish you "Good luck!" — when you finally decide whether your heart belongs to Phillips-Exeter or to N. H. C., please let us know.

name: Hodgdon, Robert Edgar
motto: Hodge was one of the charter members of the 'Don't Worry Club,' and as far as we know the only time when he is tempted to worry is when some fair co-engineer refuses to smile on him. Even our military department is a 'graft' for him. You see he belongs to that useless regimental appendage, the signal platoon. Although having but little prestige, he is in reality the cause of Prof. Porter's success as M. E. instructor. Upon considering Hodge's marvelous achievements with the 'slip stick' we feel certain that when he has reached maturity, even 'Bud' may be dethroned.

name: Hoyt, Willis Hadley
motto: All the great are dying, and I'm not feeling well
description: Would be fusser. Hard headed business man. Husky farmer and prominent Mexican athlete. Hail the conquering hero comes, tooting his own horn and leaving a trail of broken hearts behind him. Worry put the wrinkles in Bill's forehead. Why? "B"ecause: Batch, Sum, planksteaks—Alpha Zeta pin, etc., etc.

name: Hussey, Ruth Janet
motto: Cut and come again
description: Dormitory life has no charms for Ruth when home is so near, so we have only kaleidoscopic glimpses of her as she hurries to and from classes—when she really does attend them. Ruth is of an experimental turn of mind, and finds material at every side. All the mysteries of train service, jitney schedules, cut systems, and even the way to a man's heart are as an open book to her. Whatever Ruth's future may bring, we can at least be certain that it will have the charm of originality.

name: Jenkins, Harvey Foss
motto: You cannot make a crab walk straight
description: As soon as I tell you that Harvey has roomed in the Block three years, you will know that he is not quite all there. His bad habits are swearing and taking Botany 53. In fact, it is reported that he became so engrossed in research work under Ben that he forgot to go home one Christmas vacation. Jenk frequents the New Hampshire office in DeMerit Hall a good deal, but we suppose he must have business there or he wouldn't go.

name: Johnson, Ellsworth Sheldon
motto: Had he been caught young, he might have been tamed
description: Jack arrived from the wilds of "Vermont," bought a Freshman cap and proceeded to organize Rand's frat. For a year Jack was hailed as a white hope on account of his husky voice, rough-housing ability and liking for the "Good old meat and potato." He kept in training by demolishing the doors and banisters at Rand's and trying to make Buck shine his shoes. All went well until Jimmy came to town and began publishing reminiscences on the life of our hero.

name: Johnson, Myrtle May
motto: I see the right, and I approve it, too
description: Myrtle is a girl with an unusual amount of courage. That she has a mind of her own is evidenced in the fact that when the clock strikes the hour for class to be dismissed Myrtle leaves regardless of whether or not someone is still reciting. In her Freshman year she entered into college dramatics and her fondness for this art is still evident. When there is a play on Myrtle is at the "try out" and she wins, too. Along with her interest in drama she has possessed a certain enthusiasm for dancing, but this reached its height when she led the march at our Sophomore hop. Since then one of Myrtle's favorite songs has been "After the Ball Was Over."

name: Knox, Warren Penn
motto: I like its gentle gurgle, I love its fluent flow, I like to wind my mouth up And I love to hear it go!
description: This military mono-maniac hails from Madbury, "b'Jawge!" If you are centrally located on the campus you can hear "Almighty-voice" expressing his expert (?) opinion on any subject whatsoever, usually on the wrong side of the argument. Only recently he underwent a haircut, his first since he entered college. Were it not that his ears would suffer erosion, this would probably have happened sooner. Warren has earned the undying devotion and steadfast loyalty of his comrades in drill, by his soldierly bearing and military poise. This year, the Company which he commands would follow him to the ends of the earth—to kick him over the brink.

name: Lake, Leroy Moses
motto: The world knows nothing of its greatest men.
description: A sharp-witted youth, starving his body to inform his mind. This is one of them. Muddy has the honor of being the only Junior who has been mistaken and arrested as a suspicious character. Evidence was against him until he gave his name and address.

name: Lambert, Eleanor Hurst
motto: Zealous, yet modest
description: Eleanor is an out and out Arts and Science girl, with an especial leaning toward the English courses. Perhaps this explains her enthusiasm for the Book and Scroll, in which she is an active worker. This year Eleanor is contributing her share to the gayeties of dormitory life, and is a very welcome addition to our number.

name: Langley, Mary Anthony
motto: Be gone, dull care! I prithee be gone from me
description: On each morning, whether in spring, autumn or winter, Bunny drives to college from her home in the suburbs of Durham. Of course she is a little late to class, but this doesn't worry Bunny, in fact, we don't think there are many things which do worry her. For three years she and Julia have been inseparable companions, whether in class, in chapel, at home or abroad. Where one is, there is the other, also. We don't see how Bunny manages to enjoy the summer with Julia away in Ohio. At present Bunny is greatly interested in athletics, especially in athletic instruction at Dummer Academy. She even visits the academy frequently.

name: Little, Harold Benjamin
motto: Though grim as hell the worst is; he can round it off with curses
description: This quiet, timid little fellow drifted in some time ago and has been here ever since. In last year's GRANITE they put him down for a rough-houser. He doesn't like the idea of being called such, and to tell the truth he is the most peaceful guy that ever stacked a room. Hank has followed Tib and Jack for three years and has done his best to be a regular guy, but his size is against him; he is too Little, and all he can do is swear.

name: Lombard, Carl Weston
motto: 'Twas said that he could read and cipher, too
description: Lom came from Newburyport ten days late, with the intention of showing up King James. He lasted just long enough to furnish two samples of natural gas for analysis. Finding his supply unlimited, they had no further use for him and kicked him out. He landed in the Arts and Crafts division, where nature intended such men to shine. Although he does his best to prove nature wrong, his tactful manner and pleasing personality get him by his courses.

name: Long, Edward
motto: Half a loaf is better than none.
description: Ed believes half a loaf is better than none, because his Freshman year he went to a military science class without his puttees. The excuse he gave the "Lute" was that he couldn't find his puttees and that the rules required him to wear his uniform to class. He thinks he's specializing in forestry; and maybe he is, but---

name: Lord, Eugene Hodgdon
motto: "Gene" Lord is my waiter; I shall not want.
description: He maketh me to sit down at the table; he serveth me with soups. He restoreth my stomach; he feedeth me at the tables of the Crescent for "Al" Smith's sake. Yea, though I eat and eat of the soups, I am not satisfied; but "Gene" comes to me; the meat and potato they comfort me. "Gene" preparest the best before me in the presence of "Butter" Brown, who stuffeth his face with all, till his stomach runneth over. Surely goodness and prosperity shall follow "Gene" all the days of his life; and I will remember his kindness forever.

name: Meserve, Harlan Ray
description: There is considerable dispute as to where Graph originally came from; Natick claiming that he belongs in Gorham, and Gorham claiming that he belongs in Natick. There is little doubt, however, but that Miss Hillsboro will get him yet (subject to change without notice). He starred in his Sophomore year as a movie actor, but now he gets his fame as a past tutor of Eddie Mahan.

name: Morey, Isabel Marie
motto: A kind and gentle heart she had
description: After graduating from Plymouth Normal School and teaching two years, Isabel came to New Hampshire determined to burn a lot of midnight oil and graduate in three years. The last ambition she has realized, but the first has been harder to attain, as she has become popular with all the Ballardites and they won't let her study. An occasional visit from "Frank" of New London tends to upset Isabel's schedule. But through it all she maintains the sense of humor and obliging disposition which have endeared her to many.

name: Morgan, Arthur Richardson
motto: Work's an old-fashioned way of getting a living
description: Morgan's record—eight drum heads. Well, who wants this office? Morgan in the distance—Well, if nobody else wants it, I'll run. And Dick has always been in the running since, keeping about one girl behind Colomy. My uncle says— Many of us think that Dick has missed his calling and should have been a pile driver.

name: Nelson, Rufus Lawrence
motto: Yahoo doth murder sleep
description: Sleep is the staunch friend and comrade of this joyous woman-hater. If Bud Fisher or Pa Hewitt hands out a prelim, Rufe mutters, groans, staggers, and reaches for his slide-rule and then—falls asleep before the "Barr." At seven minutes before the hour he comes out of his trance, hastily writes something on his paper, attaches his John Hancock thereto, and departs perfectly happy in his pretended ignorance. Rufe seems carefree and he always has a virtuous expression on his face, for his "Soul" is unsullied by anything except Tilton mud.

name: Nichols, Earle Brazil
motto: He owneth a wicked smile
description: Nick and his beautiful smile are known all the way from Newton to Durham, but he only seems to be able to rope in the faculty with it. They all think he is a little angel, but ask him about that straw ride, Hallowe'en night. Nick is a commuter, but in spite of that has lived down the reputation attached to him because of his running mates, and has made the Field Crops Judging Team.

name: Nixon, Leonard Russell
motto: A round, vacant looking phizog, with a 'nobody home' expression, well concealed by nothing
description: Behold the champeen basket shooter of the Commuters Basketball Team. His aim is so perfect that he has been known to hit persons in the Botany Lab. with water at a distance of five feet. His Durham address is College Club Rooms, care of Muddy Lake.

name: Nudd, Henrietta Carleton
motto: A merrier maid Within the limits of becoming mirth, I never spent an hour's talk withal.
description: "Dignified, conscientious Henrietta"—well, maybe, yet a more jolly, fun-loving girl would be hard to find. She's always ready to do her part in a midnight frolic, or a quiet evening's work in the library. Henrietta's occasionally revealed theories on the problems of life show a deep thoughtfulness quite beyond the ordinary, and we feel sure that some day these same theories will do much toward making the world better and happier.

name: O'Brien, William Henry
motto: A fool there was and he made his prayer, even as you and I
description: Buck is another one of those boys that think they fit. He had a good time at the Worcester game, and we think he should have taken her to the Harvard-Princeton game. Shamus is a regular guy in spite of that, however, and he'd be the fastest man in the designing room if he didn't spend so much time helping Frank McKone write his book on gas engines. Buck has deteriorated since he got beat up in the Sophomore Poster fight; he spends too much time in the social way.

name: Parnell, George Downes
motto: An empty vessel gives the greatest sound
description: The Old Lady's first achievement was to make Rand's Frat where he soon became a favorite of Bertha's. Since then George has had several offers of Dean of Women's places. George aspired to be a hurdler, but the coach feared that he would get cast; and so he went back to Rena, and is now wondering as to what will the Weatherbee.

name: Reed, Beatrice
motto: Happiness was born a twin
description: With the coming of Bea to New Hampshire College, an element of uncertainty was added to the informals. A man, when escorting Miss Reed home from any social function, always has to ask himself this harrowing question: "Is this the twin I started with before the dance, or have I picked out the wrong one, as usual?" From such a situation, Bea is cruel enough to derive much amusement. She plays the violin and is very fond of music, especially violin duets played in Smith Hall parlor Sunday afternoons. Ask the manager of the college orchestra!

name: Roberts, Julia Aurelia
motto: God giveth speech to all, song to the few
description: "Happy" is the best word to describe Julia, for she shows her joy of living every minute, even her hair kinks with excitement. Julia's ability on the stage and the sweetness of her voice, make her an important factor of our social life; but in spite of her talent, she will probably never be a prima donna, for already she is seeing visions of a "little grey home in the West."

name: Roberts, Ray Towle
motto: Come on George! Cut the rough-housing
description: "Rabbit" was a quiet little cuss until some one told him that he was the best looking lieutenant in the regiment. That was too much for him, and as he smashed baggage before coming to college, he thought perhaps he could smash hearts. Every evening he is to be seen at the Dean's dancing class. Rabbit's essential college activity is writing E. E. reports.

name: Robinson, Nathaniel Edwin
motto: Nat, our Ballard Hall coal passer—the boy with the buxom cheeks and false teeth: the third of the Robinson episodes. Nat is very popular with the Ballard Hall neophytes, for he freely offers his assistance on wash-day. Our fair co-eds look upon him as a bashful, unsophisticated lad; but little do they know him, for, like all of Pembroke's husky N. H. C. boys, woman has no terrors for him. Besides being a connoisseur of beauty, Nat is an epicurean of faultless judgment, for he boldly and shamelessly declares his admiration for Frogs' Legs.

name: Rollins, John Hooper
description: John started his college career by annexing the high jump record and making the Glee Club. Since then he has given up his dancing class and the Glee Club in order to get by the chem. course! (?) When closely questioned, John will admit that Vernon Castle has it on him and that Caruso has a greater range.

name: Ross, Edgar Samuel
description: Sol is noted for his pawnbroker's sign from Newmarket to Rochester. Eddie started to be a mechanical bug, but the King found him wandering around the lab one day and since then Sol has been creeping about the campus with a trombone in one hand and a Chem. book in the other, trying to snare another sucker into thinking that the language of chemistry is musical.

name: Russell, Hansel Arthur
description: Hubby belongs to the noble crew of First Sergeant grafters. That band that do not sow; neither do they reap; but make out the company reports and then sit in the college club room, smoking and dreaming of those far-gone days when they, too, drilled in the ranks and were respected of their fellow-men. Hubby used to be handsome, but ever since that night when the freight refused to stop, and he had to jump, his beauty has been nil.

name: Scruton, Herbert Ambrose
motto: He stooped to conquer
description: "Dinny"—the angelic, cupid-eyed, blushing-cheeked, flaxen-haired, unsophisticated, debonair Mutt of Durham society, for all of Sid's prowess, ran him a close race, but neither finished because of their being stung by the same bee. Herbie gained his military bearing through three long years of conscientious endeavor as the hoodoo of Company A. Dinny has a wonderful wardrobe composed of everything that belongs to someone else; and perhaps his social success is due in part to this fact.

name: Shannon, Clarice Henrietta
motto: A guardian angel o'er our life presiding, Doubling our pleasures and our cares dividing
description: Oh, yes—Clarice is brainy, and enjoys the distinction of being one of the very few girls to whom "King" James ever could teach any chemistry. "King" was so surprised and pleased to find a girl who could learn chemistry that he presented Clarice with a diamond pendant. Clarice is a puzzle. Although an excellent confidante, she is also a merciless tease, and while people would enjoy her kind of sympathy, they stand in constant fear of her keen wit. However, it is too late for most of the Smith Hall inmates to complain. Clarice has them in her power! They can but trust to her kindness of heart and leave the rest to fate.

name: Steuerwald, Alberta Neal
motto: All that's best of dark and bright, Meets in her aspect and her eyes
description: Berta is very obliging, and if she were to get a commission for doing errands in Dover for the co-eds she would be driving to and from Durham each day in a "Winton Six," instead of depending upon the B. & M. or the jitney. Not only does Berta do things in Dover for the co-eds, but she also does things for her Alma Mater in the way of influencing her friends to come to N. H. C.

name: Clark Leavitt Stevens
motto: The Lord works in a mischievous way, his blunders to perform.
description: "Steve" blew in with the rest of that Colebrook gang and registered in the forestry course. Becoming a persona non grata with Tommy Schoonamaker he moved his camera and mackinaw down to "Rand's Frat" where except for tearing off the porch and pushing in a few walls, he remained in oblivion his Freshman year. However, he aspired to a theatrical career on the Dover stage but alas, one of his classmates got ahead. Discouraged by his first attempt, he retired from the stage and all that is left is the memory and the little poem in the corner of his Mem. book beginning "I didn't raise my boy to get a cabbage."

name: Joseph Abraham Sussman
description: Joe holds the long distance grouch record; he's found fault with everything in college. Although the faculty have not accepted his corrections, he lives in hopes. Joe thought he wanted to be a chemist, but the King got sick of him and kicked him out. Joe got his fame as a participant in the famous Foss-Sussman duel his Sophomore year.

name: William Hervey Thomas
motto: To know the right woman is a liberal education. —Elbert Hubbard.
description: Originally from Connecticut, he journeyed to the far north, Franconia, to be exact, where he somehow persuaded the faculty of D. A. that he knew his "stuff," in spite of his lengthy dissertations in the class room. His specialties at N. H. have been Chemistry and women. He met his Waterloo at the church reception and since that fateful evening his life has not been his own. From 5:30 A.M. till 10 P.M., he goes back and forth seeming to take an almost human interest in the basement of Smith Hall. Tom's favorite summer resort is Hampton Beach, but he doesn't seem to mind a little walk now and then on the country roads about Durham. His most important side-line is house parties.

name: Tibbetts, Carleton Briggs
description: A more innocent appearing Freshman than "Tib" never attended a minstrel show, but after getting into politics the fire-eater in disguise decided his next move should be along the social line. The brightness of Reed's Ferry society was very much to his liking, but we imagine he made a mistake when he called her "Red" because now he says "I am through with women forever."

name: Tilton, Helen Florence
motto: "She reads much; She is a great observer"
description: There's something about Helen's grave, studious face that fools the profs every time. Each labors under the delusion that all her leisure hours are spent upon his own subject, and perhaps that's why she gets her cuts excused so easily. As a matter of fact, it is notorious that, when not chasing up news items for the New Hampshire, Helen spends her time at the library, reading. Her taste in literature is varied, she takes an equal delight in the Cosmopolitan and the Psycho Analytic Review.

name: Turcotte, Abby Jewett
motto: "How doth the busy little bee Improve each shining hour"
description: How so great a quantity of energy and determination is packed into so small a body is a matter which baffles all those who know Abby. She can't get away from that keen feeling of responsibility, even long enough to let one task slip by undone. Abby appreciates the beauties of nature more than do many of us. If there is a beautiful tint in the sky or a beautiful flower half hidden by the roadside, Abby sees it and has time to stop and admire it.

name: Wagner, Edgar Richard
description: Even Hans, scientist and philosopher, is not proof against the wiles of the gentler sex. For witness, did he not transfer from Dartmouth to New Hampshire, a co-educational college; of course he might have come for the Chem. course, but—. The military is a novelty to Hans, but he likes it all, especially his battalion officers whom he would follow faithfully to battle—in hopes of getting a pot shot at them. Transferred From Dartmouth (3).

name: Wentworth, Sidney Warren
motto: Up from the meadows rich with corn, Clear on that cool September morn—
description: Came Sid, and he has been coming ever since. In fact, he was a track man in the embryo, which has since developed until now he can hold his own on the half-mile with anyone. In spite of requiring an extra large sized hat, and a few other minor deformities, such as an inability to fit with the co-eds, and possessing a rather touchy disposition, Sid is a good fellow.

name: Weston, Fred Webster
description: One of the degenerates from God's country. Still wears stripes, even in his hair. He aspires to be a second Galileo via economics. The only time he departed from the teachings of Satan, was the time he kissed his girl on the front-end of the platform at North Station. His pipe line pod developer remains a secret. He still persists in claiming that he only used one side of the road, from New Market to Durham. When Rosie plays tag, "Father Abraham" is ever it. (Everett.)

name: Weston, Helen Brown
motto: Those about her from her shall read the perfect ways of honor
description: Every morning Helen, quite simply attired, strolls in to breakfast one minute before the dining-room is closed. She has become an expert in timing her actions so that she may sleep late and then, by a hair-breadth escape, slide between those fatal curtains. Her Freshman year of college was spent at Mt. Holyoke, and if you ask her how she happened to come to N. H. C., she'll give you this bit of sage advice, which has been imparted to her by an eminent authority—"Simply a matter of combining your common sense with your knowledge, my dear." Oh, yes, one more thing about Helen: she belongs to that select group of co-eds which receive frequent and bulky letters postmarked "Hanover."

name: Westover, Kyle Chester
description: The "local boy with the Durham players" came in from the Queen City and started to crab everybody and everything and he is still at it. Meanwhile he plays football, always showing up well against Colby. When not busy crabbing or training, the dear boy labors under the delusion that he fits with Helen, Alberta, Rusty-Locks, and the Kappa cook, but O! you Grace. O! yes, our Kyle is some boy, all round. He used to snipe butts and even chewed tobacco once.

name: Whittemore, Arthur B.
description: Abe came to us with a record, but since his arrival he has established several more. His latest triumph came at Thanksgiving, when he went home a winner with Tessie under one arm and his duck under the other; the former a first in the interclass co-ed contest open to all comers; the latter a second in the cross-country race; Whit must have run some to place in both contests.

name: Rohl, Chase Wiggin
description: Cicero once told him, "You missed your calling—you should have gone on the stage telling funny stories or become a boilermaker;" and all the time he thought he was a chemist. His favorite story is—"I'll never forget the time when Pee Wee Man-ed, Dingle McClamin, and I," etc.—. Wig entered a race once, threw a shoe, stopped to be reshod, and finished third. (Note—3 in the race.)

name: Wiggin, Edwin Albert
motto: Everybody loves a fat man
description: Ed is one of our train students, and hails from Exeter. In spite of that, he has developed wonderfully, since he was a Freshman and they say he rides back and forth on the train with a co-ed.

name: Worcester, Mary Abby
motto: In each cheek appears a pretty dimple
description: In the midst of a whirl of college activities, hard work, studies and social good times, Mary preserves the serenity and calm smile which gives her the appearance of having "nothing to do till to-morrow." Neither the thought of a chemistry prelim. or of a trip to the Dartmouth-Pennsylvania football game has power to disturb her unruffled dignity. Not that Mary is indifferent to her surroundings—in fact, she takes active interest in both glee and dramatic clubs, though we often wonder how she finds time to do so much, and at the same time pretend to do so little.
